attack_surface.exposure: Data.FromRaw stores raw bytes/string verbatim; .Value triggers Materialize() on first touch, dispatching to per-(type, kind) reader registry.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: channel.read stamps {type, kind} from Mime; deferredRaw arrives unverified from any inbound channel.
mitigations: Catch-all in Materialize() (excludes NRE/OOM/SOF) converts reader failures into a touch-time Error rather than escaping into couriers.
Wire.MaxReadDepth (64) caps wire-recursion through LiftDataIfShaped/GetRawText restart paths.
Reader registry exact-match precedence (runtime > generated, exact > '*'); no string-similarity dispatch.
AsCanonical short-circuits for RawUntouched so couriers don't trigger materialize.
gaps: No size cap on captured deferredRaw at the Wire layer — bounded only by upstream channel input (Stream channel buffer-ignore is a separate standing memory item).
Catch-all is broad; a future kind reader raising CryptographicException/SecurityException would be silenced (F2).

attack_surface.exposure: Wire.Write must emit byte-identical canonical bytes for RawUntouched Data so signing.verify rehashes match the signer's hash.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Cross-actor wire — the outer Signature is the trust anchor.
mitigations: Fixed key order: name (suppressed in hash), type, value, properties, signature (suppressed in hash).
type entity has its own JsonConverter writing name/kind?/strict? in fixed order.
EmitRawVerbatim: WriteRawValue for object/json (byte-identical to GetRawText capture), WriteStringValue for other shapes (STJ canonical round-trip).
Dictionary properties iterate in .NET insertion order (deterministic per-sender).
MarkOuterForHash is ref-counted on ReferenceEqualityComparer so nested Hash composes correctly.
gaps: Non-canonical third-party wire (non-STJ writer, permuted keys, non-canonical escapes) fails verify — conservative-correct, not a gap.

attack_surface.exposure: StampReadAsync/StampValue stamp {type, kind} from channel.Mime; lazy Data flows through Variables, callstack, routing without parsing.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Inbound channel bytes from any source (file, http, socket, stream).
mitigations: File channel routes through FilePath.ReadBytes (AuthGate enforced).
Container vs value distinction is by registered serializer ownership of the Mime, not string match — no application/plang-* prefix collision.
Plang container read explicitly does NOT auto-verify (consumer's signing.verify step is the seam).
gaps: Standing: Stream channel buffer-ignore (separate memory item).

attack_surface.exposure: Per-(type, kind) reader dispatch via reflection (delegate cached on first lookup); runtime registrations from code.load-loaded DLLs can shadow generated entries.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: DLLs loaded via Assembly.LoadFrom — same accepted-risk as Module.add/provider.load (.pr is the trust boundary).
mitigations: Reflection invoke wraps TargetInvocationException to surface the inner cause.
gaps: Runtime override could replace a built-in reader silently — same as renderer.

findings.vector: Future kind reader raises CryptographicException / SecurityException; Materialize() silently absorbs and returns null with a benign Error.
findings.preconditions: A new reader integrates cryptographic verification or input-policy enforcement (none today raise these types).
findings.impact: Callers that don't check .Error before re-using the Data see Value=null with no security signal.
findings.exploit_sketch: Add a custom reader for type=secure-blob/kind=verified that throws SecurityException on policy failure. Materialize swallows it; downstream sees null and may default-pass.
findings.proposed_fix: Add the security-exception types to the not-caught list as readers that can raise them ship. Lives next to the existing NRE/OOM/SOF exclusion in Materialize().

findings.vector: Wire.Read captures the value slot as a string via JsonDocument.ParseValue + GetRawText/GetString; the string lives on the Data until first touch or variable replacement.
findings.preconditions: Inbound wire with a large signed object/<kind> value held across the goal graph.
findings.impact: Increased working-set vs. eager-then-discard. Bounded only by upstream channel input.
findings.exploit_sketch: Stream a 100MB signed JSON value through a relay actor; the deferredRaw stays in memory across the routing path.
findings.proposed_fix: Tracked under the existing standing memory item for Channel.Stream.ReadAllBytesAsync ignoring Buffer — fixing the upstream cap covers this leg.

findings.vector: Lazy parse defers parser-DoS attack vectors (billion-laughs, deeply nested YAML/XML/JSON) from channel.read time to first .Value touch.
findings.preconditions: Wire-arriving Data with type=object/yaml or object/xml and a malicious payload; receiver navigates a field.
findings.impact: Same as the pre-lazy eager-parse path — same parser, same payload, same context. Net-neutral. Verbatim relay never triggers, a small defense-in-depth gain.
findings.exploit_sketch: Pre-existing — independent of this branch. Reader-level mitigations (YamlDotNet limits, XML XXE disable) are the right layer.
findings.proposed_fix: Out of scope for this audit — reader-level concern.

summary: lazy-deserialize PASS. The signing pipeline composes cleanly with the new verbatim-passthrough leg — a relayed RawUntouched Data canonicalizes to byte-identical bytes under crypto.Hash + MarkOuterForHash, so signing.verify holds end-to-end. No auto-verify regression (parity with prior behavior). The reader registry adds a new lazy materialization seam whose catch-all is broad enough to be worth tightening as new readers ship (F2). Three low/info findings, no critical or high. Standing memory items unchanged.
